summary.intent: Review and fix a zsh plugin (`entire-fzf`) that integrates an AI session manager (`entire`) with fzf, then commit the changes.
summary.outcome: A 10-finding code review identified broken tests, behavioral bugs, and edge-case crashes. All actionable findings were fixed in the plugin and test suite, dead test code was removed, all tests passed, and the changes were committed.

repo: Functions `etfd` and `etfr` were removed from the plugin but remained referenced in `test/functions_test.zsh`, causing the test to always fail.
`_entire_checkpoint_list_by_session` was deleted; `_entire_checkpoint_table_by_session` now calls `command entire checkpoint list` directly, bypassing any shell-function stub used in tests.
`branch_filter_test.zsh` tested `_entire_branch_pick` which no longer exists — the file was dead and was deleted.
Tests are run via `test/run.zsh` and require a fake `entire` binary on PATH to simulate CLI output.
jq `//` alternative operator does not skip empty strings (`""`), only null — empty `last_active` fields need an explicit length/empty check or a computed def.
The plugin uses `xargs` to trim whitespace from TSV fields, which breaks on agent names containing single quotes — `awk gsub` is the safe replacement.

code.finding: jq `// ""` fallback doesn't skip empty-string `last_active`; fixed by introducing an `active_ts` def that uses `select(. != "")` before the null coalesce.
`claude -r "$session_id"` passes entire's session ID to Claude Code's resume flag — ID namespaces may differ; flag was not fully resolvable from the code alone.
`_entire_open_agent` catch-all `*` branch printed to stdout and returned 0 (silent success); fixed to `print -u2` + `return 1`.
No empty-check on extracted `session_id` before passing to CLI; `xargs` agent-name trim broke on quoted names — fixed with early return guard and `awk gsub`.
`select(.status != "ended")` negative filter silently admits future statuses; `first` was non-deterministic across concurrent sessions — fixed to `sort_by(.last_active) | last?`.
Loop iterated over `etf etfd etfr` but `etfd`/`etfr` are removed; corrected to `etf etfc` with explicit undefined checks for the deleted names.
Stub for deleted `_entire_checkpoint_list_by_session` was inert; replaced with a fake `entire` binary that returns fixture data.
Same dead `_entire_checkpoint_list_by_session` stub as no_color_test; removed and replaced with fake binary approach.

workflow: The `/code-review` skill runs 7 parallel finder angles (correctness, removed-behavior, cross-file, reuse, simplification, efficiency, altitude) followed by a single verify agent — effective for catching stale test/code divergence.
Fake binaries on PATH are the correct test seam when the plugin calls an external CLI with `command`; shell-function stubs are bypassed by the `command` prefix.
Requesting cleanup (remove dead loops, delete dead test files) as a follow-up after the main fix pass is an efficient workflow — avoids scope creep during the fix phase.

friction: Multiple tests were silently broken in CI due to stale references to removed functions — the divergence was not caught until the code review.
The `command` prefix in `_entire_checkpoint_table_by_session` made shell-function stubs ineffective as test seams, requiring a more complex fake-binary approach in tests.
Finding #4 (`claude -r` session ID mismatch) could not be fully resolved from static analysis alone — it depends on runtime behavior of both `entire` and Claude Code.
open_items: `claude -r "$session_id"` in `_entire_open_agent` (line 140): whether `entire`'s session ID format is compatible with Claude Code's `-r` resume flag needs runtime verification.

`select(.status != "ended")` negative filter (line 197): the sort-based fix handles the non-determinism, but the filter will silently include any new status values `entire` adds in the future — a positive allowlist would be more defensive.
